title: Timeline Dots, Semantic Lists & Account Button States
description: Fix ApplicationTimeline stage dot role='img' redundancy, convert SavedSearchesSidebar li role='button' to semantic button element, add aria-busy to SettingsAccountSection loading buttons, fix ApplicationTimeline dot role and aria-label to use aria-hidden since surrounding text conveys stage, and ensure consistent accessible state announcements.

userStories.title: Change ApplicationTimeline stage dot from role='img' to aria-hidden
userStories.description: ApplicationTimeline.jsx renders a colored stage dot with role='img' and aria-label='Stage: ${node.stage}' (~lines 57-59). The surrounding timeline list item already renders the stage name as visible text, making the role='img' aria-label redundant. Remove role='img' and aria-label from the dot span and add aria-hidden='true' instead so the decorative dot is hidden from screen readers.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationTimeline.jsx stage dot span has aria-hidden='true'
role='img' and aria-label are removed from the dot span
Stage name text in the surrounding list item is preserved
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Convert SavedSearchesSidebar li role='button' to semantic button element
userStories.description: SavedSearchesSidebar.jsx renders saved search items as <li role='button' tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={...}> (~lines 27-35). While the keyboard handling exists, using a div/li as a button is less reliable across AT than a native <button> element. Replace the role='button' li pattern by wrapping the content in a <button type='button'> element inside the <li>, then remove role='button' and tabIndex from the li.
acceptanceCriteria: SavedSearchesSidebar.jsx saved search items use a <button type='button'> inside the <li>
role='button' and tabIndex are removed from the <li>
Existing onClick, onKeyDown, and className behavior is preserved on the button
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-busy to SettingsAccountSection save/change-password button
userStories.description: SettingsAccountSection.jsx change-password submit button (~line 77) shows Loader2 when isPending is true but lacks aria-busy. Add aria-busy={isPending} to the submit Button so screen readers announce the saving state.
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsAccountSection.jsx submit Button has aria-busy={isPending}
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-busy to SettingsAccountSection delete-account confirmation button
userStories.description: SettingsAccountSection.jsx DeleteAccountModal confirm button (~line 99) shows Loader2 when isPending is true but lacks aria-busy. Add aria-busy={isPending} to the destructive confirm Button in the DeleteAccountModal so screen readers announce the delete operation in progress.
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsAccountSection.jsx DeleteAccountModal confirm Button has aria-busy={isPending}
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-busy to SettingsReportSection generate-report button
userStories.description: SettingsReportSection.jsx renders a generate report button that shows a Loader2 spinner when isLoading is true but lacks aria-busy. Read SettingsReportSection.jsx and add aria-busy={isLoading} (or the relevant loading state variable) to the generate report Button.
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsReportSection.jsx report generation Button has aria-busy reflecting its loading state
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add role='region' aria-label to NewEventForm dialog content sections
userStories.description: NewEventForm.jsx renders a dialog for adding calendar events. The dialog content area (~line 54) wraps event type, date, time, and notes fields in a generic div. Add aria-modal='true' to the dialog container (if not already set) and verify the DialogContent has proper aria-labelledby pointing to the dialog title heading so screen readers correctly announce the dialog context.
acceptanceCriteria: NewEventForm.jsx dialog has aria-modal='true' if not already set by Dialog component
DialogContent is labelled via aria-labelledby pointing to the dialog title
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-label to JobCard article for screen reader job identification
userStories.description: JobCard.jsx renders a clickable <article> without an accessible name (~line 19). After adding keyboard accessibility (tabIndex, onKeyDown in US-829), also verify the aria-label includes both role and company so the card is uniquely identifiable when screen readers list all landmark regions. Ensure the aria-label reads: aria-label={`${job.role ?? 'Job'} at ${job.company ?? 'company'}`}.
acceptanceCriteria: JobCard.jsx article has a meaningful aria-label combining role and company
aria-label is different for each card in the list
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to JobBoardContent empty-state decorative icon
userStories.description: JobBoardContent.jsx renders an empty state when no jobs are found. Read JobBoardContent.jsx and check if any decorative icon in the empty state (e.g. Briefcase or Search icon) lacks aria-hidden='true'. Add aria-hidden='true' to any decorative icons in the empty state since the surrounding text already conveys the empty message.
acceptanceCriteria: JobBoardContent.jsx empty-state decorative icons have aria-hidden='true'
Build passes with no errors
